metadata.title: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = coherent state / sloshing without spreading; CRIMSON = free packet / unavoidable spreading
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no coherent-state expansion in |n>, no Poisson photon statistics, no ladder-operator eigenvalue algebra. Wonder register.

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: A free quantum particle released as a tight packet inevitably spreads wider and wider. One specific quantum state defies this — sloshing back and forth forever without blurring.

card.copy: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading

beats.narration_text: We've seen wave packets spread: tight position means broad momentum, and components at different momenta travel at different speeds, so the packet fans out. Why should a spring-like potential change any of that?

production_viz.label: free Gaussian packet spreading over time

beats.narration_text: In a harmonic oscillator — a spring — can a Gaussian packet oscillate without spreading? The restoring force always pushes back toward center. Does that cancel the dispersion that tears a free packet apart?

card.copy: Can a Gaussian packet oscillate in a harmonic potential without ever spreading?

beats.narration_text: Yes, exactly. The coherent state is a Gaussian wave packet with width sigma equal to root of h-bar over 2m-omega — the minimum allowed by the uncertainty principle. Its center moves as A times cosine of omega-t. The width never changes.

production_viz.label: Gaussian packet oscillating in parabolic well, width constant

beats.narration_text: Why doesn't it spread? Free-particle dispersion comes from the omega-equals-k-squared relation: different momenta travel at different speeds. In a harmonic potential, the restoring force generates exactly the right phase to cancel this spreading. The shape is self-healing.

production_viz.label: free packet spreading vs coherent state self-healing comparison

beats.narration_text: The coherent state minimizes the uncertainty product at all times: sigma-x times sigma-p equals h-bar over 2, always — not just initially. No other quantum state of the oscillator does this. It is the closest quantum analog of a classical particle on a spring.

production_viz.label: sigma_x * sigma_p = hbar/2 at all times

beats.narration_text: Laser light is a coherent state of the electromagnetic field. Each mode of the field oscillates like a harmonic oscillator. The photons in a laser are in a coherent state: definite phase, amplitude uncertain but phase locked. This is why laser beams maintain their shape.

production_viz.label: laser mode as coherent state, definite phase, maintained shape

beats.narration_text: Put a coherent state in a harmonic potential with its center displaced by A at time zero. At one quarter period, the center reaches zero and momentum is maximum. At half period, it returns to minus A. At one full period, exactly back to A — width unchanged throughout.

production_viz.label: coherent state at t=0, T/4, T/2, T — width constant

beats.narration_text: Classical harmonic motion and the coherent state differ in one way: the coherent state has a minimum width set by h-bar. As h-bar approaches zero, the width approaches zero and classical mechanics is recovered. The coherent state is the quantum world's closest approach to the classical.

production_viz.label: coherent state width ~ sqrt(hbar/2momega), hbar->0 limit

beats.narration_text: Free particle: always spreads. Harmonic potential coherent state: oscillates at omega, minimum-uncertainty width, never spreads. The spring heals the blur. Laser light lives here.

card.copy: Free packet: spreads. Coherent state: oscillates at ω, width frozen at minimum uncertainty. Spring heals the blur.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
